title: Woman injured in Natick during marketplace meet-up to sell $27,000 diamond
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Keyjuan Webb allegedly attempted to steal a $27,000 diamond during a marketplace meeting at Natick Mall.
Kathleen Sanfacon was dragged and injured while trying to stop Webb; she was hospitalized with hand, elbow, and hip injuries.
Webb faces multiple charges, including unarmed robbery and assault; he was held without bail pending a dangerousness hearing.

title: He’s running Boston to support a family friend’s fight against cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Sean Pruneau, 32, is running the 130th Boston Marathon for Dana-Farber with Team Dennis in support of Dennis Ford.
Dennis Ford, a longtime charity organizer, was diagnosed with glioblastoma and became a Dana-Farber patient in August 2023.
Team Dennis, inspired by Ford's fight, aims to fundraise for cancer research and promote a world without the disease.

title: Boston hotels increasing pricing, programming for the World Cup this summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston hotels are increasing prices by over 300% around World Cup opening matches with specialized programming and events.
FIFA World Cup will host seven games at Gillette Stadium, rebranded as Boston Stadium, expected to draw over 2 million visitors.
Boston hotels are offering themed packages, such as XV Beacon's $12,000 luxury suite and Hotel AKA's Golden Goal Getaway.

title: ‘Violent fugitive’ wanted in Lawrence murder arrested in Maine
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jean Michael Warner Delgado, 20, arrested in Baldwin, Maine, for a Lawrence homicide during a welfare check.
Delgado is charged with murder and firearm offenses related to the death of Hugo Delrosario on Sept. 13.
Delgado is held in Cumberland County Jail awaiting extradition to Massachusetts for court arraignment.

title: Jayson Tatum will miss Celtics game against Thunder
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum is out for the Celtics' game against the Thunder due to managing a right Achilles injury.
The Celtics have a 2-1 record since Tatum's return, defeating the Mavericks and Cavaliers but losing to the Spurs.
Payton Pritchard will return from neck spasms, while Derrick White is questionable with a right knee contusion for the game.

title: Boston City Council moves toward potential kratom ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council considers banning kratom after hearing testimony on its health risks and connection to fatalities.
Kratom products, containing the compound 7-hydroxymitragynine, are sold in forms like tablets and drink mixes in Boston.
Over 20 Massachusetts municipalities have banned kratom, with state legislation pending for broader regulation.

title: ‘I was overcome’: Conan O’Brien tells Jimmy Kimmel about ’emotional’ trip to Ireland
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien traveled to Galbally, Ireland, visiting his great-grandfather's former home for an episode of his HBO Max show.
O'Brien described the visit as 'emotional,' highlighting his empathy for immigrants and the challenges faced by his great-grandfather.
On 'Jimmy Kimmel Live!', O'Brien discussed his upcoming role hosting the 2026 Oscars and reflected on his Irish heritage trip.

title: MBTA bus crashes into parked cars, home in Medford
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: An MBTA bus crashed into parked cars and a home's steps on Playstead Road in Medford early Sunday morning.
The bus driver reported losing control due to black ice before the bus hit two parked vehicles and the home's steps.
MBTA Transit Police are investigating the incident; no further details have been provided by police at this time.

title: Here are the richest people in Massachusetts, according to Forbes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Forbes listed nearly 30 Massachusetts-based billionaires in its latest World's Billionaires List, with combined global fortunes totaling $20.1 trillion.
Boston Celtics owner Bill Chisholm and GE Aerospace CEO Larry Culp are new Massachusetts additions to the billionaire rankings.
Abigail Johnson, with $33.2 billion, tops the list of Massachusetts billionaires, followed by Robert Kraft and Edward Johnson IV.

title: ‘I am a Raider’: Maxx Crosby breaks silence after Ravens trade falls through
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maxx Crosby trade to the Ravens fell through due to concerns about his medical status.
Crosby posted on social media confirming his commitment to the Raiders after the trade collapse.
The Patriots have been linked to Crosby but would need to evaluate his health before trading.
